title: Prism3 Theme Schema
description: The brand-defining input contract for the Prism3 generation engine. A brand is a small validated input set; the engine expands it into the full token tree, AI metadata, and platform outputs. Discipline: start at the five required inputs, expand to six on a strong need, resist the seventh — fewer than five causes visual collapse, more than six grows engine surface non-linearly.

schemaVersion.description: Version of this contract the input targets. Used for cascade analysis and deprecation windows.

primaryColor.description: REQUIRED #1. The brand's primary color as an OKLCH seed. The engine treats this as a fixed anchor and generates the full contrast-anchored ramp AROUND it (not from a hue). The supplied value is PRESERVED EXACTLY — it appears verbatim as a ramp step, never normalized to the generator's nearest lightness target (the Univers lesson: most tools omit the real brand value and force manual re-insertion). The default ramp is ~20 steps (025..950); step count is an engine constant, not a schema input. The engine also derives contrast-pinned text-on-primary pairs and accent semantics by formula.

name.description: Human label, e.g. 'Acme Blue'. Flows to docs and .ai.json.

chromaCeiling.description: Optional per-brand override of the generated hue's max chroma, to keep vivid steps in-gamut. Defaults to the engine's per-hue ceiling.

brandColors.description: OPTIONAL. Additional brand colors beyond primaryColor, for brands with more than one brand hue (e.g. a couple of brand blues). Each value is PINNED EXACTLY (per primaryColor). The engine groups colors of a compatible hue (deltaH within ~15-20deg) that sit at distinct lightness positions into a SINGLE multi-anchor ramp that passes through all of them; colors that diverge in hue, collide at the same lightness, or conflict in chroma each get their OWN named scale. The grouping is automatic; set `scale` on an item to force it. Fit-or-split changes how many ramps carry the values, never the values.

neutralHue.description: REQUIRED #2. Seed for the gray ramp. Carries the brand's temperature (cool/warm/neutral). The engine generates a full neutral ramp tinted toward this hue.

hue.description: OKLCH hue angle the neutrals are tinted toward.

chroma.description: How saturated the neutrals are. Near 0 = true gray; higher = visibly tinted.

typography.description: REQUIRED #3. Typeface stack by ROLE. Family-role indirection is the white-label type lever: a font swap is a single primitive change, no composite rewritten. The engine fills core.font.family.{display,text,mono} from these and generates the modular scale, weight ladder, and fluid triplets.

display.description: Headlines / expressive tier. Defaults to the text stack if omitted.

text.description: Body / UI tier. The one required stack.

mono.description: Code / tabular tier. Defaults to a system mono stack if omitted.

scaleRatioText.description: Modular scale ratio for the text tier (e.g. 1.200 Minor Third).

scaleRatioDisplay.description: Display pivot: separate (larger) ratio for the display tier. A single uniform ratio fails at the extremes.

scriptFallbacks.description: Optional per-script family overrides (cyrillic, cjk, arabic, …). Latin pairing harmony does not transfer across scripts; each is re-validated. Emitted as DTCG modes / @font-face unicode-range.

radius.description: REQUIRED #4. The form-factor distinguisher (sharp = industrial, soft = consumer). A single scalar that scales the whole corner system from none to full.

scale.description: Multiplier on the base radius ramp. 0 = fully sharp (all corners collapse to 0 except pill/full); 1 = system default; up to 2 = very soft.

baseMd.description: Optional anchor (px) for radius.md before scaling. The rest of the ramp is derived.

density.description: REQUIRED #5. The spacing-rhythm step. Parameterizes the spacing scale; generated and committed as literals per density mode (never a runtime expression). Application-default with modality-as-signal — not a runtime contract.

default.description: The brand's default density. 'compact' maps each semantic spacing step one dimension-step tighter.

steps.description: Which density modes to generate. Two by default; add a third only with analytics justification.

secondaryColor.description: OPTIONAL. A second brand hue. Generated as its own contrast-anchored ramp.

accentColor.description: OPTIONAL. An accent/highlight hue, distinct from primary action color.

statusColors.description: OPTIONAL and DISCOURAGED. Status colors are infrastructure, not brand assets — they stay perceptually stable across brands so 'error' always reads as error. Override only with a deliberate reason. Omitted = engine defaults. When the brand primary sits in red territory, the engine carves a separate destructive red regardless of this field.

displayP3.description: OPTIONAL. Emit Display-P3 render targets (alongside sRGB hex fallback) for brand-critical surfaces such as heroes and accents.

motionPersonality.description: OPTIONAL — the disciplined 6th input. Brand easing/duration character. Reduced-motion variants are always generated regardless (vestibular durations drop to 0).

easingEmphasized.description: Optional cubic-bezier override for the emphasized easing curve.

brand.description: OPTIONAL metadata. Drives docs, .ai.json, and the per-brand MCP surface. Does NOT produce token values.

id.description: Stable slug, used for output package naming and the data-brand attribute.

voice.description: Short tone-of-voice note; informs $description / when_to_use in .ai.json.

oklch.description: A color in OKLCH — the authoring space for Prism3. L 0-1, C 0+ (per-hue ceilings apply), H 0-360.

brandColor.description: A brand color seed. The oklch value is preserved exactly as a ramp step (anchor). Optionally controls how it groups into ramps.

name.description: Human label, flows to the generated scale name, docs and .ai.json.

scale.description: OPTIONAL grouping control, overriding the engine's automatic fit-or-split. A named scale id (e.g. 'brand-blue') forces this color onto/into that ramp as an additional anchor; 'separate' forces its own standalone scale. Omit to let the engine decide by hue/lightness distance.

fallbacks.description: Ordered fallback families; the full stack lives in the token, never in consumer CSS.

variable.description: Is this a variable font? If true the engine freezes named weight instances at the token layer.

axes.description: Optional declared variable axes (wght, wdth, opsz, slnt, GRAD). When opsz is present the engine sets font-optical-sizing: auto by default.
